title: Lineara candidate-equation hypothesis (v1)
description: A *local* hypothesis: a span of signs in one inscription is proposed to spell a candidate substrate root. Scored by the local_fit_v0 metric. Distinct from the existing global sign->phoneme mapping shape (hypothesis.v0); this shape is additive.

schema_version.description: Discriminator. The harness loader dispatches on this field.

name.description: Stable slug; should match the file basename. Mixed case permitted so inscription IDs (HT, ZA, KH, ...) read naturally.

created.description: ISO-8601 date the hypothesis was authored.

source_pool.description: Informational tag for the substrate pool the root was drawn from (e.g. 'aquitanian', 'pre_greek_toponym', 'linear_b_carryover'). The full pool ingest is a separate ticket; this field carries the intent.

surface.description: Surface form of the candidate root (orthographic).

phonemes.description: Ordered phoneme sequence. Length must equal the number of entries in equation.sign_to_phoneme.

citation.description: Where the root was sourced (Trask 1997 page X, Gorrochategui 1984, Beekes 2010, etc.).

span.description: Inclusive [start, end] over the inscription's tokens[] array. Both indices must lie within tokens; signs at non-syllabogram positions (DIV, LOG:, FRAC:, [?]) are not picked up by sign_to_phoneme.

sign_to_phoneme.description: Ordered (Python 3.7+ insertion-order) sign->phoneme map. Keys are the corpus sign tokens that fall within span; one entry per phoneme in root.phonemes, in the same order. Mapping non-syllabogram tokens is not allowed.
